GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "Why a gloss is the best thing to happen to your hair in ages", "Redken's masterful ammonia-free hair colour, Shades EQ Gloss, explained by a pro.", "Great hair colour has superpowers. It can change your outfit, your mood and your day. There are, of course, a multitude of techniques that a professional can employ to optimise the hair colour you were born with, from balayage to babylights. But perhaps the most exciting innovation to come out of the investigation into hair colour that doesn't sacrifice hair health is Redken's in-salon service, Shades EQ Gloss. "It's an ammonia-free colour, which glazes over the hair adding both shine and condition, while enhancing or neutralising certain tones in the hair," says hairstylist Danny Luu, from Oscar Oscar Salons in Brisbane, who regularly uses the ingenious formula on clients. Redken's Shades EQ Gloss is demi-permanent, suits all textures, and is available in a staggering scope of shades, all with the same uber-glossy, sheer result. Whether you're new to colour or frequently switch things up, whether you want to dabble in pastels or turn up the dial on your existing colour, whether you want to nix brassiness between appointments or just want a shimmering shine, there's a shade of gloss to match. Here, Luu explains the standout traits of Redken Shades EQ Gloss and why we could all use a little more gloss in our lives. Low key colour that fits in with you. Whether you're softening greys, toning, correcting or just giving some oomph to a lacklustre hair colour, the finish of Shades EQ Gloss is sheer, so the result is natural. You, but better— without committing to a high-maintenance regimen you'll regret later. "Shades EQ Gloss is 'non-committal' colour—it gradually fades while growing out which means it doesn't create a hard demarcation line. This ensures that it's low maintenance from the very first colour," explains Luu. It turns up the shine. Redken Shades EQ Gloss works more like a conditioner than a colour, with potent actives designed to care for the health of hair—important given the things we expect our tresses to withstand in the modern world. "I always love how much more shiny and conditioned the hair is after a gloss. The shine makes the hair look healthier, and often people will comment before we've even dried the hair off! I've also had guests come back saying that their hair was so much easier to manage and style at home, because it was so conditioned," explains Luu. Built for speed. Processing time for a gloss is only 20 minutes. It feels more like a treatment than a colour service, so can even be done in the basin. "We're all time poor. Getting the most out of your appointment in the salon is crucial," says the hair pro. Luu says if you don't have time for a full colour service in the salon, a gloss between treatments can stretch out your appointment schedule, without affecting the health of your strands. As for which of the Shades EQ Gloss colour to try next, Luu says that one particular decade and its trends are coming back. "There's a return to colours that are more defined and reminiscent of the '90s' though I don't think stripey highlights will ever be back in," laughs Luu. "Hair colour and cuts are so much more personalised, with a plethora of techniques that we can use on hair now. Much like make-up, we use dark to contour and light to highlight and accentuate," Luu adds. Whatever colour you choose, blinding shine, the kind you get from a gloss, never goes out of style. To book your Shades EQ Gloss treatment at Oscar Oscar Salons click here. For more examples of how Oscar Oscar Salon's award-winning salons can optimise your colour, visit their Instagram."
